reviews.review_description:
Update: After the dev's reply I could past that section of the game, maybe I am not the brightest as I misunderstood the game instruction - but I would replace "Hold thumbstick button in" to "Move the joystick down and hold" in case there are other not so bright people like me.

developer_response.body:
Hey TayoThor! Thank you for your comment!
The issue is that you’re clicking the thumbstick. The message says “Hold thumbstick down,” not “Hold thumbstick button in.”
You just need to move the thumbstick downwards and hold it without pressing it in.
If you continue to have any problems, feel free to message me at anomosstudios@gmail.com

Update: Hey TayoThor! I’m glad to hear the issue has been resolved.
If you encounter any other problems with the game, just send me an email at anomosstudios@gmail.com
  I’ll be happy to assist you!
Thank you very much.
Hey Rambrocious, Thank you very much for your comment! This is really great that you liked the game.

reviews.review_description:
Bionic Hunter could be a hidden gem. The world design is the best part, in my opinion. The graphics are realistic and well-textured, the music is rad, combat is mostly smooth (I have some confusion regarding reloading, which I'll get to in a bit), and the immersion is strong. I've paid more for less for other FPS titles. There are a few small changes that could be addressed.

Some areas for improvement:

- there are simply too many words on the screen, which really reduces immersion. Is there a setting to eliminate those? While you're at it, is there a way to eliminate the numerical scores that pop up once one has killed an enemy? Those don't necessarily affect gameplay as much, but they reduce immersion and realism nonetheless. In any case, please include a setting to reduce or eliminate all the onscreen prompts and kill scores.

- I don't understand reloading. What actually reloads the gun? Pressing the B button, or collecting ammunition? Where does the collected ammunition go? Does it fully reload all guns, or just one type? What does "ready to reload" (or whatever it says) mean? A tutorial here would be helpful.

- minor aesthetics: the screen contrast seems to be very low in general, and then seems to get even lower at times. The blacks need to be darker in general; the worlds have a slightly faded grey appearance. Also, I'm not sure if it's supposed to be a day/night pattern, but there are times at which my screen seems to randomly fog/grey out to the point that I can't really see anything. It's very disorienting and doesn't seem intentional.

Overall, this is a really fun game and it's clear that the highly responsive devs are dedicated to making something that they're proud of. A few small tweaks would put it into the top echelon of FPS games.

developer_response.body:
Hey QUACKBIRTH!! This is really great that you liked the game. Thank you for your message. I have already responded to all your questions in the email I sent you.
To summarize here as well:
-About the Kill Scores: There is an option to disable them in the settings.
-About reloading: You can reload by clicking the reload button, which fully reloads your current weapon. If you pick up an ammo pack, it refills all of your weapons. When your ammo reaches zero, trying to shoot again will automatically reload your weapon (this auto-reload feature can also be disabled in the settings).
-About the screen contrast issue ("the screen contrast seems to be very low in general, and then seems to get even lower at times"): This is a visual damage effect caused by enemy projectiles.
-About the many messages: You can disable them from the settings — this turns off around 90% of the in-game messages.

I'm here if you need anything else or have any further questions!

reviews.review_description:
Bionic Hunter: The Ancient Sword is one of the most polished and thoughtfully designed games on the Meta standalone platform. It blends fluid gameplay with clever mechanics and throws you into missions that stay fresh—whether you're destroying generators, hunting down enemies, or tracking a legendary sword.

The enemies are wonderfully grotesque, each with their own look and strategy, making every encounter feel distinct. You can tell the studio poured love into this game—every detail feels carefully crafted, from mission variety to visual design. It plays and looks closer to a PCVR title than most standalone games.

If you're looking for something original, smart, and engaging, Bionic Hunter delivers. Can’t wait to see what this dev team releases next!

This is an amazing game that I absolutely recommend! The gameplay is smooth and engaging, and the visuals are breathtaking. It’s clear a lot of care went into the design, making it a must-play for anyone looking for a fantastic gaming experience.

developer_response.body: Hey Ntalia! Thank you for your review with 5* rating. We're very glad that you liked the game.

reviews.review_title: One guy did this? Get out!
reviews.review_description:
I tried Shock Troops first and thought it was okay. Clearly an homage to Castle Wolfenstein. But Bionic Hunter is Quake (which I preferred).  Not exactly scary (except sometimes the monsters sneak up behind you and the big ones are BIG) and the environments are nothing special and a bit repetitive, but man, those monsters look beautiful.   As others have said there are missions to keep it from being just repetitive wave shooting and I hope more are added. I agree with the comments that the pop-up messages are annoying. Even though there is an option to disable them some still come through and are very obtrusive. I don't need to be reminded 20 times that I have to repair two more satellites, thanks!

I am truly in awe that only one guy was able to put out such a good game, and I agree it belongs on the official store!

Response to dev: Awesome! Can't wait to check out the new stuff. Keep up the really great work!

developer_response.body:
Hey MisssElaineous, Thank you for your review. I am glad that you liked the game.
Τwo days ago released new Huge Update!New Missions are available on map 2 and  map 3,  added new Monsters new Weapons , Bow! you can climb! you can take control of your robot in third person! some messages related to missions cannot be disabled.

Once you know the controls it’s so good to play.the developers have pulled out all the stops on this one this should be on the main section of the oculus store better than a lot of other games on there by far.so if u like a great shooter this is right up your street a co-op mode would take this to the next level if that’s a possible upgrade for the future fingers crossed a big thumbs up to the game  devs :)

reviews.review_title: low budget but amazing Doom clone
reviews.review_description:
I can't believe this is even running on Quest, some genuinely impressive graphics showcase, good texturing but mainly a display for normal and specular mapping like you rarely see in Quest games. Had they baked shadows into the maps this would look even more impressive.

As for the game itself, it's very fast-paced shooting with no time for manual reload, but I'd still would've enjoyed it there was the popular shake weapon to reload. They also feel mostly weightless, lack some punch compared to other VR shooters, but I can't quite pinpoint what it is. For all the good presentation and lots of demons, it feels a bit on the easy side. I like that maps are reasonably open and offer an element of exploration, it's not an arena wave shooter.

One thing I really hate though: the tons of messages popping up all the time in some of the most cheesy fonts and colors ever - some even superimposed onto previous ones! This is awful, please just have a control help menu or at least a menu option to turn off all such messages.

developer_response.body: Hey namekuseijin, thank you for your review. It’s very nice to know that you liked the game. On a future update i add option to  turn off all messages.

reviews.review_description:
Video Review - https://youtu.be/SpR2MRv9QQI

Bionic Hunter : The Ancient Sword a game where your sent to an Alien planet with the task to survive waves, so yes this is a wave shooter at its heart but its been given a lot of love to make it feel like so much more. Speaking literally first is well ME yes I volunteered to record some lines for this game earlier this year so you get to hear me, I am not going to judge my own voice acting but I will say that having spoken lines makes the game feel a lot more polished and immersive to engage yourself into the situation. The game also has full smooth locomotion options including turn speed so its very adaptable to your playstyle and feels great to move around.

The game doesn't stop there though, Bionic Hunter has a huge cast of very creepy looking aliens that look straight out of Doom or an Alien horror movies from the 80s. Lots of creepy walking animations, and a huge scale which can really look terrifying up close. 

On top of the Aliens though we can collect the souls of fallen monsters after a mission involving finding and repairing Satellite's. Once we get 10 souls we can trade them in and call for a robot ally to come and kick some serious Alien butt! Having the extra help adds a lot to the experience for me and I love seeing the robot swing the sword and let me catch my breath.

Bionic Hunter is a fantastic game at a low price point with a lot to play with and be excited about. The wide variety of weapons and abilities gives you the chance to swap your play style constantly to avoid being bored and all the monsters keep it exciting to keep progressing and see what is unleashed on you. I am very excited to have my voice in the game which definitely adds to my personal enjoyment but as a game its just one that is very easy to pick up and play and have a good time.

developer_response.body: Hi Lipnox, and thank you for your video review, It’s very nice to know that you liked the game.

reviews.review_description: This is a wave shooter with Story Elements to it.  It has amazing graphics, grotesque monsters and a variety of weapons across 4 different worlds.  Def recommend this one.  Here is a link to a video I did on it.  https://youtu.be/J3luACFB-AY

reviews.review_description:
This is a blast to play.

Reminded me of Doom/Quake.

Graphically the volcano world (the only world I've ventured to so far) is quite impressive in places. Animation on the creatures could be better, but they're still mostly impressive.

Gun handling is a bit of a problem, feels flimsy holding a weapon compared to most games of this genre. Doesn't feel like I'm holding the weapon.

There are tons of weapons to choose from.

I didn't actually like the swords at all, and preferred to stick with the guns. When fighting the creatures with the sword, it didn't feel like I was hitting them.

Still, the positives very much outweigh the negatives, and this comes highly recommended.

reviews.review_title: OK except a bug with map loading
reviews.review_description: The game looks OK so far but... I started by playing the "volcano" map, failed at mission 7 or so. Then wanted to start again from lobby by loading "volcano" map but the "ice planet" has loaded instead. If I select "ice planet" to load, then "ice planet" will load as well. Retried few times, I just cannot reload the "volcano" map again. Why?

developer_response.body:
Hey Plytro,  I am glad that you liked the game. 
It is not bug, you must complete the Missions on ice planet  and get your new weapon (Bow!)  on the first two levels on the map ice , after that you can choose any map  that you have Unlocked!

reviews.review_description: Look out Quake and Doom, there's a new kid in town.  This game is pure and simple fun, but still challenging and chaotic at times, as monsters come at you from all directions.  The weapons are cool, the graphics and gameplay are excellent, and the dev continues to update and improve the game on a regular basis, giving us more options to choose from.  100% worth the asking price, and it should be directly available to purchase on the Oculus store to begin with, as it's so much more deserving to be than other games that are for some reason.   10/10.  :)
